title: Recover every interrupted commit deterministically
description: Every candidate-granular crash point must resume from durable manifest, receipt, and LEDGER idempotency state without duplicate effects.
target: 100% of injected interruption points before LEDGER call, after LEDGER commit, before receipt durability, after receipt durability, and between candidates recover to a complete receipt in which each candidate is accepted once, returned as the exact prior result, or explicitly unresolved or conflicted; zero second canonical transactions.
load_condition: Every candidate outcome class, batches with multiple candidates, lost stdout, process termination, receipt write failure, and repeated resume.
measurement: Published-process fault-injection matrix with direct LEDGER reference round-trip and receipt/cardinality assertions.
rationale: Candidate-granular commit is safer than batch atomicity only when the uncertain crash window is closed by exact replay.
